topic: FL Studio workflow standards for assistant planning

rules.recommendation: Read current channels, playlist tracks, mixer tracks, patterns, and routing before proposing cleanup.
rules.rationale: FL Studio supports multiple valid workflows and does not enforce one-to-one Playlist/Mixer/Channel mapping.

rules.recommendation: When names/colors already indicate linked Instrument or Audio Tracks, keep Channel, Playlist Track, and Mixer Track naming/coloring consistent where safe wrappers exist.
rules.rationale: The documented linear workflow creates linked naming, coloring, channel, playlist, mixer, and pattern context.
safety_limits: Only update surfaces exposed by rollback-safe tools.
Do not infer a link from numeric index alone.

rules.recommendation: For Channel Rack workflow projects, infer mixer relationships from channel target mixer tracks and names rather than playlist track positions.
rules.rationale: The manual states Playlist Clip Tracks are universal lanes and Channel Rack to Mixer routing decides processing.
safety_limits: Do not assume Playlist Track N maps to Mixer Track N.
Read back every applied routing change.

rules.recommendation: Treat drag-and-drop routing, plugin loading, effect insertion, external inputs, and hardware outputs as manual guidance unless a safe MCP wrapper exists.
rules.rationale: The routing video demonstrates UI workflows; the project safety contract requires snapshot, write, readback, changelog, and rollback for persistent MCP writes.
safety_limits: External hardware routing should be reported as manual guidance.
